programme.title: The Blue Realm Ep112: Deep White - Why Sharks Attack
programme.desc: Deep White explores ways to research sharks to determine why sharks attack and to study innovative ways to train sharks in order to prevent future attacks. Sharks are surprisingly intelligent and able to perform complex tasks. Sharks can distinguish different signals and cues in feeding routines and they develop strong bonds with their trainers.

programme.title: The Great Barrier Reef: World's most amazing coral reef
programme.desc: This expedition to the Great Barrier Reef is designed to study the health and resilience of one of the world’s most amazing coral reefs. programme.title: Next Stop Ep111: Tahiti
programme.desc: This Next Stop episode explores the three Tahitian Islands of Moorea, Huahine and Bora Bora via Quad rides, cultural hikes, jet skiing, exploring a Pearl Farm, shark & stingray feeding, and by checking out some of the best resorts as well as the local music scene.

programme.title: The Blue Realm Ep110: The Ship Sinkers
programme.desc: Mothballed, de-commissioned naval vessels are toxic time-bombs, filled with PCB’s, asbestos, lead paint, oil and grease. Hundreds rust in dockyards around the world. What can be done with them? A team of Canadians has developed an innovative solution…they clean the ships...then sink them!

programme.title: All Stars
programme.desc: A group of 5 All Star contestants were chosen to embark on an adventure overseas, but what they didn’t know is that it would become a maritime mission. Radio Host Kerri Kasem and rockstar Ashba join Don as he breaks the news to the contestants that there’s more to this yacht than a pleasure cruise. If they thought that this trip was going to be smooth sailing, they soon realized how mistaken they were when Don hit them with the hardest physical evolution in Surviving Mann’s history.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: Surviving Mann returns with its most punishing season opener yet. In true Don Mann fashion, the bar is raised with a grueling 10-part physical readiness test—infused with a blast of hypothermia for good measure. Don himself calls it “the most brutal PT [he’s] ever witnessed,” and it’s clear the contestants are in for a world of hurt. To push them even further, MMA legend Ken Shamrock joins the fray, bringing his no-nonsense intensity to whip the group into fighting shape. Those who survive the trial by ice and exertion are rewarded with a chance to test-fire the lightest AR on the market—if they can still feel their trigger fingers.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: For the first time in Surviving Mann history, the contestants undergo intense combat training led by none other than Ken Shamrock—"the most dangerous man in the world." His relentless situation drills push this elite group beyond their limits, sharpening their instincts and forging next-level tactical readiness. As a new mission begins to take shape, the pressure mounts—and the team learns that the strongest among them will earn a game-changing reward. But in a world where every edge matters, not everyone is built to keep up. Fatigue sets in, tensions rise, and the difference between teammate and competitor begins to blur. With Shamrock watching closely, only the most disciplined will rise to meet the challenge—and prove they’re worthy of what lies ahead.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: Back by unpopular demand, the infamous “Blah Line” returns—and it’s more brutal than ever. The contestants kick off the day with a hellish PT session that leaves bodies broken and spirits tested. Things only get rougher from there with a punishing stealth crawl over jagged rocks, grinding them down before they face Ken Shamrock’s infamous Lion’s Den training—a relentless gauntlet of pain, grit, and raw endurance. As Don Mann continues to forge a battle-ready team, the squad is thrown into breaching drills designed for high-stakes mission execution. It’s chaos, it’s calculated, and it separates the contenders from the pretenders. In the heat of the action, two standouts emerge from the ranks—earning the coveted role of designated breachers.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: Only a few days in, the 32 are rapidly progressing through their training for the upcoming mission. This episode includes several challenges including live ammo run and shoot, barriers and cover and an extreme rope course. Can the leader stay on top? Or will someone else pull ahead?

programme.title: Journey Through Joshua Tree Relaxing Nature Film
programme.desc: Explore a wonderland of unique desert life in "Journey Through Joshua Tree." programme.title: The Napa Valley - A Journey through America's Wine Country
programme.desc: The Napa Valley" features the spectacular landscape photography of Olof & Elizabeth Carmel paired with beautiful piano and guitar instrumentals. This 42 minute video is a relaxing and inspiring journey into the beauty of Americas Wine Country - The Napa Valley. It features the seasons of the Napa Valley, from spring wildflowers and blossoms through the rich colors of autumn in the vineyards.

programme.title: Autumn River

programme.title: Meditating in Winter
programme.desc: Experience a relaxing guided meditation led by Mark Coleman paired with inspiring winter scenery.

programme.title: Arches & Canyonlands Ambient Nature Film
programme.desc: The sublime landscape of Arches and Canyonlands area of Utah is the star in this 90 minute film, which showcases awe-inspiring natural arches, dramatic canyon vista sunrises & sunsets, and flights through arches and over the Colorado River canyon. The spectacular cinematography is accompanied by beautiful Native American flute music. Note: All Drone footage was filmed outside of National and State Park boundaries.

programme.title: Replay: Grace & Glory
programme.desc: REPLAY: Grace & Glory is a faith-based program airing Sunday mornings at 7am on WMAR. The program features weekly inspirational sermons from local ministries, interviews with gospel artists and community interest segments.

programme.title: Natural Disasters
programme.desc: This week we see how science helps us prepare for natural disasters. Philippe heads to the University of Miami to see their incredible SUSTAIN lab that can simulate a category 5 hurricane. Then it's off to the mountains of Alberta to learn about avalanche safety. From there's we head to South Carolina to follow researchers that study floods. And finally at the University of Reno a "shake table" helps with designing buildings that can hold up during earthquakes.

programme.title: Taking Flight
programme.desc: This week we take flight within the animal kingdom. Philippe joins a researcher to go into Congaree National Park in South Carolina to catch and tag local bats. We then look at the flight patterns and tendencies of wood storks. And finally from caterpillar to butterfly we examine the transformation from crawling to flying.

programme.title: The Danny Moses Show
programme.desc: Danny Moses ("The Big Short") cuts through the market noise to simplify the week's financial headlines and trends, with unique insights and special guests.

programme.title: Keystone Species
programme.desc: This episode features a variety of animals found on land, and in the sea. Find out how the anteater is able to eat nearly 10,000 insects every day. And why the walrus is considered a keystone species in the Pacific Northwest.

programme.title: Natural Disasters
programme.desc: This week we see how science helps us prepare for natural disasters. Philippe heads to the University of Miami to see their incredible SUSTAIN lab that can simulate a category 5 hurricane. Then it's off to the mountains of Alberta to learn about avalanche safety. From there's we head to South Carolina to follow researchers that study floods. And finally at the University of Reno a "shake table" helps with designing buildings that can hold up during earthquakes.

programme.title: CBS 6 News Weekends
programme.desc: Get the latest breaking overnight news and the weather to plan your day with a little fun along the way. Join Greg McQuade and Meteorologist Mike Stone weekend mornings from 6 to 8 a.m. on CBS 6 News.

programme.title: Replay: CBS 6 News Weekends
programme.desc: Get the latest breaking overnight news and the weather to plan your day with a little fun along the way. Join Greg McQuade and Meteorologist Mike Stone weekend mornings from 6 to 8 a.m. on CBS 6 News.

programme.title: Natural Disasters
programme.desc: This week we see how science helps us prepare for natural disasters. Philippe heads to the University of Miami to see their incredible SUSTAIN lab that can simulate a category 5 hurricane. Then it's off to the mountains of Alberta to learn about avalanche safety. From there's we head to South Carolina to follow researchers that study floods. And finally at the University of Reno a "shake table" helps with designing buildings that can hold up during earthquakes.

programme.title: Taking Flight
programme.desc: This week we take flight within the animal kingdom. Philippe joins a researcher to go into Congaree National Park in South Carolina to catch and tag local bats. We then look at the flight patterns and tendencies of wood storks. And finally from caterpillar to butterfly we examine the transformation from crawling to flying.

programme.title: Route 66 Road Trip
programme.desc: Take a drive with Photowalks host Jefferson Graham along the famous Route 66. He'll give photo trips for an epic road trip.

programme.title: Keystone Species
programme.desc: This episode features a variety of animals found on land, and in the sea. Find out how the anteater is able to eat nearly 10,000 insects every day. And why the walrus is considered a keystone species in the Pacific Northwest.

programme.title: Natural Disasters
programme.desc: This week we see how science helps us prepare for natural disasters. Philippe heads to the University of Miami to see their incredible SUSTAIN lab that can simulate a category 5 hurricane. Then it's off to the mountains of Alberta to learn about avalanche safety. From there's we head to South Carolina to follow researchers that study floods. And finally at the University of Reno a "shake table" helps with designing buildings that can hold up during earthquakes.

programme.title: Keystone Species
programme.desc: This episode features a variety of animals found on land, and in the sea. Find out how the anteater is able to eat nearly 10,000 insects every day. And why the walrus is considered a keystone species in the Pacific Northwest.

programme.title: Natural Disasters
programme.desc: This week we see how science helps us prepare for natural disasters. Philippe heads to the University of Miami to see their incredible SUSTAIN lab that can simulate a category 5 hurricane. Then it's off to the mountains of Alberta to learn about avalanche safety. From there's we head to South Carolina to follow researchers that study floods. And finally at the University of Reno a "shake table" helps with designing buildings that can hold up during earthquakes.

Philippe heads to the University of Miami to see their incredible SUSTAIN lab that can simulate a category 5 hurricane. Then it's off to the mountains of Alberta to learn about avalanche safety. From there's we head to South Carolina to follow researchers that study floods. And finally at the University of Reno a "shake table" helps with designing buildings that can hold up during earthquakes.

programme.title: Keystone Species
programme.desc: This episode features a variety of animals found on land, and in the sea. Find out how the anteater is able to eat nearly 10,000 insects every day. And why the walrus is considered a keystone species in the Pacific Northwest.

programme.title: Natural Disasters
programme.desc: This week we see how science helps us prepare for natural disasters. Philippe heads to the University of Miami to see their incredible SUSTAIN lab that can simulate a category 5 hurricane. Then it's off to the mountains of Alberta to learn about avalanche safety. From there's we head to South Carolina to follow researchers that study floods. And finally at the University of Reno a "shake table" helps with designing buildings that can hold up during earthquakes.
